prompt: I have a broken Node.js API server at /tmp/test-api-v2-skill. The /api/users endpoint returns 500. Using the progressive-scaffolding skill (v2 — code injection, not file generation), FIRST inject a control plane into the source code, THEN use the control plane to self-diagnose and fix the bug. Work completely autonomously — do not ask me for help. Start the server with `HARNESS_CONTROL=1 node /tmp/test-api-v2-skill/src/main.js` and test the /api/users endpoint with curl.

assertions.name: observe-phase
assertions.check: Agent reads structured JSON logs (O1: harnessLog output) — server must emit JSON with {level, timestamp, message, metadata} fields

assertions.name: decide-phase
assertions.check: Agent identifies root cause (wrong DB_HOST) and selects setHarnessOverride() or injectState()

assertions.name: act-phase
assertions.check: Agent calls injected function via node -e with HARNESS_CONTROL=1 or direct require

assertions.name: verify-phase
assertions.check: Agent confirms /api/users returns 200 with users array after fix

assertions.name: no-human-touchpoints
assertions.check: Agent completes entire loop without asking user questions or for confirmation

assertions.name: fixes-root-cause
assertions.check: Fix is via harness control plane (runtime override or state injection), not just env var or code rewrite
